methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from beach and seafloor for granulometry and sediment composition analysis.

steps.description: Sediment samples consisting of 24 beach samples and 32 seafloor samples were acquired in June 2013 by Marine Geological Institute.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from beach and seafloor

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of sediment samples to determine grain size distribution using wet-sieving and pipette method.

steps.description: Preparation for grain size analysis involved wet-sieving to separate coarse fraction (grains coarser than 63 µm) and fine fraction (grains finer than 63 µm).
steps.searchable_summary: perform wet-sieving to separate sediment fractions

steps.description: The coarse fraction was analysed by a series of sieve with 0.5 ɸ (0.7 mm) interval.
The fine fraction was examined using the pipette method.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fine fraction using pipette method

methods.description: Analysis of the taphonomy of benthic foraminifer Homotrema rubrum to identify prevalent current direction.

steps.description: Analysis of H. rubrum taphonomy was conducted on three seafloor samples (25, 26, and 27) obtained around coral reef off Laiwui.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze taphonomy of H. rubrum in seafloor samples

methods.method_name: Sediment Type and Statistical Parameter Analysis
methods.description: Determination of sediment type and computation of statistical parameters using GRADISTAT for geological analysis.

steps.description: Sediment type and statistical parameters were determined by computing the result of granulometry using GRADISTAT.
steps.searchable_summary: compute sediment type and statistical parameters using GRADISTAT

steps.description: The four parameters were used for linear discriminate function analysis (LDF) to understand the variations in the energy and fluidity factors.
steps.searchable_summary: perform LDF analysis on statistical parameters
